warningSignsRecognition.description: Key indicators that someone may be in crisis
verbalSigns: Talking about wanting to die or kill themselves
Expressing feelings of hopelessness or having no reason to live
Talking about feeling trapped or in unbearable pain
Talking about being a burden to others
behavioralSigns: Withdrawing from activities and social contact
Increased use of alcohol or drugs
Sleeping too much or too little
emotionalSigns: Sudden calmness after a period of depression
Loss of interest in most activities

deEscalationTechniques.name: Active Listening
deEscalationTechniques.description: Give full attention, reflect feelings, and validate their experience without judgment.

deEscalationTechniques.name: Grounding Techniques
deEscalationTechniques.description: Use the 5-4-3-2-1 technique: identify 5 things you see, 4 you touch, 3 you hear, 2 you smell, 1 you taste.

deEscalationTechniques.name: Safety Assessment
deEscalationTechniques.description: Directly but compassionately ask about suicidal thoughts, plans, and access to means.

deEscalationTechniques.name: Collaborative Safety Planning
deEscalationTechniques.description: Work together to create a plan for staying safe in the immediate future.

safetyPlanningSteps: List people and social settings that provide distraction
List people to call for help
List professional resources and emergency contacts

emergencyResources.name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
emergencyResources.description: 24/7 free confidential support for people in suicidal crisis or emotional distress

emergencyResources.name: Crisis Text Line
emergencyResources.description: Free 24/7 crisis support via text message

emergencyResources.name: SAMHSA National Helpline
emergencyResources.description: Free referral and information service for mental health and substance abuse

copingStrategies.name: Deep Breathing Exercise
copingStrategies.description: Controlled breathing to activate the parasympathetic nervous system

copingStrategies.name: 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding
copingStrategies.description: Sensory awareness technique to manage overwhelming emotions

copingStrategies.name: Progressive Muscle Relaxation
copingStrategies.description: Systematically tensing and releasing muscle groups
steps: Start with your feet - tense for 5 seconds

copingStrategies.name: Thought Defusion
steps: Say 'I notice I am having the thought that...'
Visualize the thought as words on a screen
Watch the thought float away like a cloud

copingStrategies.name: Safe Place Visualization
copingStrategies.description: Creating a mental image of a calm, safe environment
steps: Close your eyes and take deep breaths
Imagine a place where you feel completely safe
Engage all senses in the visualization
Stay in this place for several minutes
Slowly return your attention to the present
